name: PHP Version Manager (Tauri + Rust + React)
description: Cross-platform GUI to install, switch, and remove PHP runtimes with checksum-verified downloads, PATH management, and atomic operations with rollback.
longDescription: phpvm targets developers who juggle multiple PHP versions: a Tauri shell hosts a React UI that lists installed and available releases, tracks download cache with checksum verification, switches active versions with PATH updates, and removes unused builds. Rust on the backend provides memory-safe file operations with rollback support, while the interface exposes progress reporting and settings for mirrors and cache locations.
features: Download and install official PHP builds with integrity checks
One-click activation with automatic PATH updates
Tabbed UI for installed, available, cache, and settings views
Cross-platform focus with primary support for Windows and Linux
highlights: Combines Rust safety with a modern React interface
Operational focus: checksums, atomic installs, rollback
challenges: Filesystem and PATH mutations are risky on end-user machines; mistakes brick shells or leave partial installs.
solutions: Atomic operations with rollback, verified checksum pipeline, and explicit UX around destructive actions.
security: Avoid running the GUI with elevated privileges unless necessary
